revision.comment: Bot: mark 2 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:今泉口八幡山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun |image = [[File:Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun, enkei.jpg|280px]]
Distant view of the mound (front part on the left, rear circular part on the right) |Alternative name = Morita Village Mound No. 12 |Cluster = Suga-no-Sawa Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Higashi Imaizumi Town (Ota City)|ja|東今泉町 (太田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Imaizumi Town (Ota City)|1=Higashi Imaizumi Town (Ota City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116679639}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|qid=Q386179|lt=Ota City|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 46.10 |coordinates = 139|coordinates = 22|coordinates = 40.90 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length: 60 meters |burial = {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) |burial goods = Earrings, straight swords, short swords, handguards, iron arrowheads, cylindrical silver-bronze products, coil-shaped gold-bronze products, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Satellite Tombs = |built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Gunma |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-west |map_dot_label = Imaizumiguchi
Hachiman-yama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun''' (今泉口八幡山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Higashi Imaizumi Town (Ota City)|ja|東今泉町 (太田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Imaizumi Town (Ota City)|1=Higashi Imaizumi Town (Ota City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116679639}}, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|qid=Q386179|lt=Ota City|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. This keyhole-shaped tumulus is part of the {{ill|Suga-no-Sawa Kofun Cluster|ja|菅ノ沢古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga-no-Sawa Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135579220}}. It has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == The tumulus was constructed on the southern slope of a branch hill of the Kanayama Hills in eastern Gunma Prefecture. It is situated at the northern end of the Suga-no-Sawa Kofun Cluster, with the {{ill|Suga-no-Sawa Gobyō Kofun|ja|菅ノ沢御廟古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga-no-Sawa Gobyō Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135579221}} located to the north{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. The mound and the stone chamber have suffered significant destruction, but excavations were conducted in {{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} and {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} (Heisei 6–7). The tumulus has a well-developed front section facing westThe "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" initially described the mound as having its front section facing east, but the Ota City Board of Education revised this in their investigation report to face west, placing the stone chamber in the rear circular section.. burial goods such as {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (including asagao-type haniwa) and {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} (depictions of people, horses, houses, shields, etc.) have been unearthed, possibly sourced from the {{ill|Kita-Kanai Komagata Shrine Haniwa Kiln Group|ja|北金井駒形神社埴輪窯跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Kanai Komagata Shrine Haniwa Kiln Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. Surrounding the mound, a moat 2.5 meters wide and 1 meter deep has been identified on the north side of the rear section{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear section, opening to the south. It measures 10 meters in total length and contains a {{ill|House-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Despite evidence of looting, burial goods recovered include earrings from within the sarcophagus and straight swords, short swords, handguards, iron arrowheads, cylindrical silver-bronze products, coil-shaped gold-bronze products, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} from the chamber{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the later stages of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun built|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Ota City History, General Edition: Prehistoric and Ancient|1996}}{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. == History == * Genbun 3 (1738): Documented in the "Nitta Kanayama Stone Sarcophagus Inquiry Report"{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. * {{ill|1938|en|1938|qid=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13): Listed as "Morita Village Mound No. 12" in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran." * {{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} and {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Excavations conducted during retaining wall construction for landslide prevention measures on the southern side of the tumulus. Report published by the Ota City Board of Education in {{ill|1996|en|1996|qid=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}. == Mound area == The area of the tumulus are as follows{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: Estimated reconstruction at 60 meters * Rear circular section diameter: Estimated reconstruction at 29 meters * Front section width: Estimated reconstruction at 50 meters == burial == [[File:Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Remaining Stone Chamber}}]] The burial facility is a {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed in the rear circular section, with an opening facing south. Its area are as follows{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * burial chamber (gen-shitsu): 5.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, 2.4 meters high * Passageway (sendo): 4.8 meters long, 1.3 meters wide The chamber's construction materials include {{ill|Chart (disambiguation)|en|Chart (disambiguation)|qid=Q1067738|lt=Chert|1=Chart (disambiguation)}} and Kanayama stone (welded tuff){{Sfn|Ota City History, General Edition: Prehistoric and Ancient|1996}}. Traces of white clay coating remain between the chamber's rear stones and ceiling stones{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. A house-shaped sarcophagus made of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} is located inside the chamber. The sarcophagus is oriented east-west, perpendicular to the chamber's main axis. Its area are{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Lid: 1.91 meters long, 1.05 meters wide, 0.45 meters high * Body: 1.85 meters long, 0.97 meters wide, 0.54 meters high The lid features two rope-loop projections on the passageway side{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. burial goods recovered include: * From the sarcophagus: Two gold-bronze earrings * From the burial chamber: Straight swords, short swords, handguards, iron arrowheads, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (goblets and jars) * From the passageway floor: Cylindrical silver-bronze products and coil-shaped gold-bronze products{{Sfn|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. == References == {{References}} {{reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used for writing this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|title=Ota City History, General Edition: Prehistoric and Ancient|publisher=Ota City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ota City History, General Edition: Prehistoric and Ancient|1996}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Miyata Takeshi|year=2002|chapter=Hachiman-yama Kofun > Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|title=Su11lement to the Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|publisher=Tokyo-dō Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|Sources not used for writing this article}} * {{Cite book|year=1996|title=Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun Excavation Report - Excavations Associated with Landslide Prevention Measures at Imaizumiguchi|publisher=Ota City Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite book|year=1997|title=Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|publisher=Ota City Board of Education|ref=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun}} [[snIma Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|version=99996026|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:今泉口八幡山古墳]] {{Commons category|Imaizumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122190137}} [[Category:History of Ota, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
